zh.Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾
zh.Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United King: 英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林
zh.Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, Uni: 英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特卡德洛斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de Isaac於1306年出生在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林時，他的父親Thomas Isaac32歲，母親Isabel Matilda Mar32歲。他於大約1328年與Princess Matilda Maud Bruce在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1363年在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪被列為town clerk。他去世於1353年7月20日在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾享年47歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭費伏。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas de Isaac于1306年出生在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林时，他的父亲Thomas Isaac32岁，母亲Isabel Matilda Mar32岁。他于大约1328年与Princess Matilda Maud Bruce在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1363年在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪被列为town clerk。他去世于1353年7月20日在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾享年47岁，埋葬在苏格兰費伏。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Isaac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, and French: from the Biblical Hebrew personal name Yishaq ‘he laughs’. This was the name of the son of Abraham (Genesis 21:3) by his wife Sarah. The traditional explanation of the name is that Abraham and Sarah laughed with joy at the birth of a son to them in their old age, but a more plausible explanation is that the name originally meant ‘may God laugh’, i.e. ‘smile on him’. Like Abraham , this name has always been immensely popular among Jews, but was also widely used in medieval Europe among Christians. Hence it is the surname of many gentile families as well as Jews. In England and Wales it was one of the Old Testament names that were particularly popular among Nonconformists in the 17th–19th centuries, which accounts for its frequency as a Welsh surname. (Welsh surnames were generally formed much later than English ones.) In eastern Europe the personal name in its various vernacular forms was popular in Orthodox (Russian, Ukrainian, and Bulgarian), Catholic (Polish), and Protestant (Czech) Churches. It was borne by a 5th-century father of the Armenian Church and by a Spanish martyr executed by the Moorish rulers of Cordoba in AD 851 on account of his tireless polemics against Islam. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Iskhaq or Ishak , Hungarian Izsák, and Slovenian Izak, and also their patronymics, e.g. Serbian Isakov . The name Isaac is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Issac .

variants.name: Abraham
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Mathai, Mohan, Raju, Saji, Prasad, Anil, Babu, Biju, Leela, Sajan, Shibu, Suma.

English, French, Spanish, German, Slovenian, Ethiopian, and Assyrian/Chaldean; Hungarian (Ábrahám), Slovak (mainly Abrahám, also Ábrahám), Czech (also Abrahám): from the Hebrew personal name ʾAbraham (modern spelling Avraham), borne by the Biblical patriarch revered by Jews as the founding father of the Jewish people (Genesis 11–25) and by Muslims as founder of all the Semitic peoples, both Hebrew and Arab (compare Ibrahim ). The original name of the Biblical patriarch was probably Abram , meaning ‘high father’ (from ab father, ram ‘exalted’), while its later form is explained in Genesis 17:5 as being derived from Hebrew ab hamon goyim ‘father of a multitude of nations’. It was widely used as a personal name among Christians as well as Jews in the Middle Ages. The name Abraham is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. As a surname of Hungarian origin Abraham is also found in Romania. — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, the Ethiopian name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Gaelic Mac an Bhreitheamhan ‘son of the judge’. See McBroom and compare Breheny .

variants.name: Isakson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Alf, Knut.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Isaksson: patronymic from the personal name Isak (see Isaac ). This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian and Danish cognate Isaksen . Compare Isackson .

variants.name: Ishaq
meanings: Arabic and Assyrian/Chaldean: from the personal name ’Isḥāq, Arabic and Syriac form of the Biblical name Isaac , which is also the name of a prophet in Islam (see the Koran 37:112). This form of the name is most common among Muslims in the Indian subcontinent and Nigeria. Among Assyrians/Chaldeans the name is also commonly spelled Iskhaq. Compare Ishak .

variants.name: Isaksen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nils, Borg, Gunner.
Norwegian and Danish: patronymic from the personal name Isak (see Isaac ). Compare Isackson and Isakson .

variants.name: Isak
meanings: Jewish: variant of   Isaac  .
German: variant of   Isaak  .

variants.name: Isaacson
meanings: Jewish and English: patronymic from the Old Testament personal name Isaac + -son. In North America, this surname has probably also assimilated cognate names from other languages.

variants.name: Iskhakov
meanings: Muslim (territory of the former USSR) and Jewish (from Uzbekistan): patronymic from the personal name Iskhak, equivalent to Isaac , with the Russian possessive suffix -ov.

variants.name: Isaacs
meanings: Jewish, English, and Dutch: patronymic from the Old Testament personal name   Isaac  . This surname is also established in South Africa. It is rare in the Netherlands.

variants.name: Isaak
meanings: Jewish: variant of   Isaac  .
German: from the personal name Isaak, German form of Isaac .

variants.name: Isakov
meanings: Muslim, Jewish (from the territory of the former USSR), Russian, Serbian, and Bulgarian: patronymic from the personal name Isak (see Isaac ), with the Slavic possessive suffix -ov.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de Isaac was born in 1306, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Isaac, was 32 and his mother, Isabel Matilda Mar, was 32. He married Princess Matilda Maud Bruce about 1328, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. In 1363, his occupation is listed as town clerk in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. He died on 20 July 1353, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in Fife, Scotland.

fr.Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, Uni: Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de Isaac est né en 1306, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Isaac, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Isabel Matilda Mar, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Princess Matilda Maud Bruce environ 1328, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. En 1363, sa profession de son indiquée est town clerk à Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 20 juillet 1353, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Fife, Écosse.

de.Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, Uni: Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas de Isaac 1306 in Dunfermline, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Isaac 32 und seine Mutter Isabel Matilda Mar 32. Er hat um 1328 in Dunfermline, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom Princess Matilda Maud Bruce geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. 1363 ist sein Beruf als town clerk in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 20. Juli 1353 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Fife, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de Isaac è nato il nel 1306 nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas Isaac, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Matilda Mar, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Princess Matilda Maud Bruce circa 1328, nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie. Nel 1363, la sua occupazione è indicata come town clerk nel Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Morì il 20 luglio 1353, nel Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Fife, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Isaacが1306年にイギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインで生まれた時，父Thomas Isaacは32歳，母Isabel Matilda Marは32歳であった。約1328年にイギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインでPrincess Matilda Maud Bruceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1363年，イギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーンでの職業はtown clerkと記録されている。彼は1353年7月20日にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアで47歳で死去し，スコットランドフィフにて埋葬された。

ko.Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, Uni: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 카드로스
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Isaac(이)가 1306년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Isaac(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Matilda Mar(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1328년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 Princess Matilda Maud Bruce(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1363년, 그의 직업은 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 town clerk이었다. 그는 1353년 7월 20일 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 피프에 묻혔다.

pt.Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, Uni: Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de Isaac nasceu em 1306, em Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Isaac, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Matilda Mar, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Princess Matilda Maud Bruce aproximadamente 1328, em Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas. Em 1363, sua profissão aparece como town clerk em Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 20 de julho de 1353, em Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Fife, Scotland.

ru.Cardross, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, Uni: кардросс, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de Isaac родился в 1306 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Isaac было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Matilda Mar, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Matilda Maud Bruce около 1328 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. В 1363 году он работал по профессии town clerk в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Он умер 20 июля 1353 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности фиф, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de Isaac nació en 1306, en Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Isaac, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Isabel Matilda Mar, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Princess Matilda Maud Bruce aproximadamente 1328, en Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas. En 1363, su ocupación registrada era town clerk en Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Murió el 20 de julio de 1353, en Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Fife, Scotland.
